title: Why no computer thinks 0.1 + 0.2 = 0.3 #Shorts
desc: Ask almost any computer for 0.1 + 0.2 and it won't say 0.3 — it says 0.30000000000000004. It's not a bug: computers store numbers in binary, and one tenth has no exact binary form (just like one third never ends in decimals), so the total rounds a hair too high. The same IEEE-754 rounding shows up in Python, JavaScript, C, and almost every device you own.
